author: @unda25
text: nice audio quality :))

author: @neatt3815
text: I have a coolant leak on my car. Unfortunately, no easy or cheap fix for me. It's leaking from the thermostat housing... it's located behind the engine. Thanks Holden for putting an engine designed for a fwd car into a rwd car 🙄

author: @oliver90owner
text: Masking tape wrapped round tightly a few times is good enough.  The important thing is not to pressurise the system.  Drive sensibly with the rad cap just cracked open.  End of problem until a proper repair can be effected.

author: @djough1
text: Well, there's your problem! It's pretty obvious to me anyway. They put the drivers seat on the wrong side of the car! Just kidding from across the pond. Good video. Cheerios!

author: @shaunmarriott4118
text: Dude if you live that close to work just walk save on fuel and there’s no risk of coursing any more damage to your car come on man get a grip. Great fix for an emergency tho

author: @TechKnowCSS
text: YOU CAN JUST SLIDE A METAL PIPE INSIDE THE HOSE AND HOSE CLAMP IT FROM OUTSIDE ON BOTH SIDES OF THE HOLE. WONT LEAK ANY AND WILL LAST MUCH LONGER.

author: @Teknopottu
text: Nice video. Having some strong tape, steel wire and a pair of pliers in your car can save your road trip and save you alot of money to keep things together until getting it properly repaired.

author: @Teknopottu
text: Nice video. Having some strong tape, steel wire and a pair of pliers in your car can save your road trip and save you alot of money to keep things together until getting it properly repaired.

author: @seahorseist
text: Awesome !

author: @rayg436
text: I have had the same problem on the side of the road. I used electrical tape on the hose and duct tape on top of that for strength. This patch lasted 3 months of continuous driving. Without any leaks. At the 3 months  time I put on the new one

author: @YoureNowOnTV
text: The 'Giza' car lives to drive another day ! 😀👍

author: @stevenfaber3896
text: these are both AC options, but hair dryer/heat gun to warm the rubber to flex itself again and allow for you to refuse the slit?

author: @docink6175
text: when it works it works... Im confused as to why it needs to go to the shop when you have the hose off... just buy a new one and replace it, top up with coolant and save $$$

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Yeah for sure! I did end up replacing it myself, as the hose, I ordered come a bit quicker than the garage could have booked me in for. it is all done now though. Thanks! :)

author: @theimpatientbrewer
text: You took the hose off, so why not just fit a new one and save some money?

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: at that point, the part I needed wouldn't be in stock for about a week. so it was ordered, but just needed to do somthing just to get me to work and back.

author: @glenparker234
text: Not that good of and idea I had a flat from a nail in Barstow California it was around 130 degrees the patches lasted around a half hour to an hour before coming off your radiator runs a lot hotter than that so you better shut it off before it hits normal operating temperature and let it cool back down it would probably help taking off the radiator pressure cap to keep it from building up pressure use a rag to keep the water from bubbling out it helps. It wouldn’t hurt to wrapping a couple layers of duct tape around the hole it has strings in it and will hold up to the pressure and the heat a lot better than the patches but I would still leave off the pressure cap and don’t let it get to hot before letting it cool down

author: @edwardhughes352
text: good tip

author: @angelusmendez5084
text: Great! 👏

author: @BoB4jjjjs
text: You would have been just as well fitting the proper pipe yourself, you had it off anyway!

author: @herenow2895
text:
Why didnt you just buy a new one and replace it yourself, rather than book it in somewhere ?
But the emergency roadside, get you home job was good.

author: @whatnextjoe
text: Cool idea Luke, Thanks for sharing, Oh you might want to check your camera it shows the driver seat on the right side of the car (strange)

author: @OverUnity7734
text: Same, I was in the middle of the desert once and radiator hose burst .  All I had was red cellophane tape meant to temporarily patch a break light  lense . It slowed down the leak enough and I was able to drive another 30 miles before all the water boiled out and the engine got too hot .  Still in the desert and I knew a rest stop was 50 miles away .  I made the difficult decition to use my drinking water in the radiator and I wrapped cloth around the cello tape to give it more support . Drove slowly, about 50 MPH, and the last 10 miles the radiator was dry, engine dangerously hot, I feared I had damaged the engine.  I made it to the rest stop and filled all my water containers . The rest of the way I only drove at nite and not over 50, a day and a half later I made it to California and was able to buy a new hose .  The desert is no joke, those cactus dont have more than a drop of water in them, I checked !  Thought I was going to die that day. Never drove a car again with out having spare radiator hoses in the back .

author: @dexterconnolly3696
text: That was quite well done bro. I wonder if a segment of bike tyre tube would hold up instead of the pipe

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: That is a really cool thought for sure, I can imagine if you used it as a sleeve that could work quite well, but I wouldn't be sure lol. Thanks mate! :)

author: @AverLife
text: Why spend money booking it in, when you can replace the pipe, with one from the scrapyard.

author: @ThisRandomUsername
text: You should also probably drive with your heater on full and your fan running as hard as possible. That will make sure the engine doesn't get up to temperature, keeping the pressure in the hoses down. Also, as Stu C says, crack the pressure cap open too.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: This is an awesome tip! I wish I said this on the video, that's what I had to do when I put the initial fix on the pipe, worked a charm, and kept the engine cool! Thank you mate! :)

author: @stubluesjhb1
text: Get them all changed.  If you do do a temp fix, crack the pressure cap to keep the system pressure lower and drive slower

author: @MAGnetICus_Attractus
text: I helped a person jump start their car and noticed I had hose ballooning, used half a roll of electrical tape to reinforce it. Forgot about it until it burst and regretted not spending $3.57 for a new hose.

author: @MrAnderson4509
text: it's hard to believe how many times people came in to the store looking for a sort of saddle patch clamp repair device for their pipes, the frequency was high enough that I noticed and then all of a sudden two people came in 15 minutes apart looking for the same thing, so good timing on this

author: @Titanium369
text: nice video. I once had a nail in my tire that wouldn't hold air. 30 miles from home.  I had a number 2 pencil and half a can of fix a flat.  replaced the nail with the pencil and broke the pencil off flush. filled the tire as much as possible with the fix a flat inflate. drove 25mph- but made it home.   Then, quickly placed the spare back where it should have been hours ago.🤣

author: @stevetobias4890
text: I used to cut the pipe at the leak and stretch it to fit. This only works when the bends allow for a bit of give. Otherwise you can sometimes open the split to push a bit of rubber (or silicon) into it then tightly wrap it with electrical tape. Also the patch won't hold pressure unless you patch it inside and outside.

author: @themechanic7625
text: You wouldn't have that problem if you did better maintenance on your vehicle, and black electric tape works just as good

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Hahaha agreed, I only just got the car back from the garage with the coolant system replaced, the only thing they didn't do was the upper radiator hose (the one in this video) but they replaced the bottom radiator hose. i think it was down to bad luck tbh LOL.
